message:
chore(beads): close ATC comprehensive test coverage epic (br-c68hj)

All 5 sub-beads completed:
- br-1u8gy: DecisionCore edge cases (5 tests)
- br-w5phh: EvidenceLedger + AtcDecisionRecord edge cases (5 tests)
- br-oco5x: AgentRhythm + Liveness boundary conditions (7 tests)
- br-ems9z: Martingale engine + CalibrationGuard (by other agent)
- br-kgew5: SessionSummary + LoadRouter + Predictive (by other agent)
- br-30ydx: E2E live server integration test (5-phase)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-bhqq4","title":"Fix am robot project resolution, beads discovery, and reservation scoping in nested workspaces","description":"Observed during manual code exploration of mcp-agent-mail-cli.

Scope:
- Resolve project from CWD ancestors so nested workspace paths work for `am robot` commands.
- Include `--project` in follow-up `am robot thread` actions.
- Discover `.beads` from nested directories instead of hardcoding cwd/.beads/beads.db.
- Include deferred/other counts in `am beads status`.
- Scope reservation conflicts/expiring lists to the selected agent when not using `--all`.

Verification:
- `cargo fmt --check` via rch
- targeted `cargo check`/`clippy` via rch
- note any external blockers separately","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:41:32.665530042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:45:13.031101751Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:45:13.030028532Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed nested project/beads resolution, thread follow-up project scoping, reservation scoping, and beads status accounting","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","cli","robot"]}

 {"id":"br-brsks","title":"ATC Track 4: Evidence Ledger — Decision Audit Trail","description":"# Track 4: Evidence Ledger

## Graveyard Source: §0.8 Evidence Ledger pattern, alien-artifact-coding §2

## What
Every ATC decision is logged with full context: the posterior belief distribution,
the loss matrix consulted, the action chosen, the expected loss, and the evidence
that led to the decision. This makes the ATC fully auditable and debuggable.

## Why
An ATC that makes invisible decisions is worse than no ATC. When the ATC
releases an agent's reservations, the operator needs to understand WHY.
The evidence ledger answers: "What did the ATC see, what did it believe,
and what would have happened if it chose differently?"

## Data Model
```rust
struct AtcDecisionRecord {
    /// Unique decision ID
    id: u64,
    /// Timestamp of the decision
    timestamp_micros: i64,
    /// Which subsystem made the decision
    subsystem: AtcSubsystem, // Liveness | Conflict | LoadRouting | Synthesis
    /// The entity the decision concerns
    subject: String, // agent name or thread ID
    /// Posterior belief at decision time
    posterior: Vec<(String, f64)>, // [(state_name, probability)]
    /// Action chosen
    action: String,
    /// Expected loss of chosen action
    expected_loss: f64,
    /// Expected loss of the next-best alternative (for "how close was it?")
    runner_up_loss: f64,
    /// Key evidence that drove this decision
    evidence_summary: String,
    /// Whether the calibration guard was healthy at decision time
    calibration_healthy: bool,
    /// Whether safe mode was active
    safe_mode_active: bool,
}
```

## Storage
The ledger is stored in-memory as a bounded ring buffer (last 1000 decisions).
It's also surfaced through the existing event system (MailEvent) so the TUI
can display it and the git archive captures it.

No new DB tables — the ledger piggybacks on the existing event ring.

## Evidence-to-Message Bridge
When the ATC takes a non-trivial action (not "ignore"), it sends a message
to the affected agent AND logs to a #atc-decisions thread:
```
[ATC Decision #42] Declared agent BlueFox as Suspect.
Evidence: no activity for 847s (avg interval: 120s, 5.8σ).
Posterior: P(alive)=0.12, P(flaky)=0.41, P(dead)=0.47.
Action: suspect (expected loss: 3.2, vs. declare_alive: 18.1).
Probe sent at {timestamp}. Will escalate to Dead if no response in 240s.
```

## Dependency
- Requires Track 1 (Decision Core) — logs decisions from the core
- Independent of Tracks 2/3 — can be built in parallel once Track 1 exists

## Files
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/atc.rs` (ledger module within ATC)

## Tests
- Unit: decision records are correctly formatted
- Unit: ring buffer evicts oldest when full
- Unit: evidence summary is human-readable
- Unit: runner_up_loss is correctly computed from loss matrix
- Integration: decisions flow through to MailEvent ring","status":"completed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:54:04.365698672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T21:27:22.099087860Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-brsks","depends_on_id":"br-emhdw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:54:04.661349200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-c68hj","title":"ATC: Comprehensive test coverage — unit tests, edge cases, and E2E integration","description":"# ATC Test Coverage Hardening

## Problem
The ATC has 101 unit tests covering the happy paths, but significant
edge cases, boundary conditions, and integration scenarios are untested.
The E2E test script (test_atc.sh) only checks CLI flags — it doesn't
exercise the ATC inside a running server with real DB queries.

## Scope
Three categories of missing coverage:
1. **Unit test gaps** — edge cases and boundary conditions for existing code
2. **Missing feature tests** — windowed session summaries (bead spec but not implemented)
3. **E2E integration** — ATC running inside a live server with real events

## Success Criteria
- Every public function/method has at least one dedicated test
- Edge cases (zero, negative, overflow, empty) covered
- E2E script exercises the ATC with real server + DB
- All tests use sqlite:///:memory: (no real DB flakiness)
- Detailed logging on failure (full state dump)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:10:19.170795888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:14:05.130462333Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-c68hj","depends_on_id":"br-1u8gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:10:39.594605591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-c68hj","depends_on_id":"br-30ydx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:14:05.098674113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-c68hj","depends_on_id":"br-ems9z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:11:55.322249859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-c68hj","depends_on_id":"br-kgew5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:12:24.984205438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-c68hj","depends_on_id":"br-oco5x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:11:30.241943123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-c68hj","depends_on_id":"br-w5phh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:11:03.543551940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Problem

 {"id":"br-cfqsc","title":"Agent Mail runtime: SQLite 'database disk image is malformed' blocks inbox/ack/send","description":"## Problem\nAgent Mail CLI operations (, , ) now fail with  in /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust.\n\n## Impact\n- Multi-agent coordination is blocked (cannot send/ack/fetch reliably).\n- Bead/messaging workflow cannot proceed through Agent Mail even though code compiles/tests.\n\n## Repro\n1. \n2. Observe error: .\n\n## Suspected scope\nfrankensqlite/sqlmodel runtime path or corrupted project DB state under concurrent writers.\n\n## Needed\n- Root-cause analysis for malformed image trigger.\n- Repair/migration path for affected DB.\n- Regression tests for send/ack/list-acks under concurrent conditions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CalmAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:01:19.228756654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:04.714802897Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:04.714778651Z","close_reason":"Implemented DbPool startup corruption guard: quick_check detects malformed primary SQLite, auto-restores from healthy sibling backups (.bak/.backup-*), quarantines corrupt DB + sidecars, and continues startup. Added targeted unit tests; verified am mail inbox/send now works on default project DB.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-cncyt","title":"fix: robot inbox ORDER BY + search-core compilation + clippy sweep","notes":"Fixed 3 infrastructure issues: (1) Robot inbox query ORDER BY column resolution (6a263ac), (2) search-core compilation break from 866b6bd2 wrapped fast_embedder Option at 15 sites (0ae5882), (3) 26+ clippy lints across 6 crates (c4a759a). All workspace crates clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-23T03:53:20.860778003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-23T03:53:26.028106570Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T03:53:20.860778003Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-cu0th","title":"Investigate Beads DB integrity mismatch: br doctor passes while raw sqlite integrity_check reports comment/index corruption","description":"After repairing br-uaa06 by rebuilding and promoting a fresh canonical .beads/beads.db from synchronized issues.jsonl, the user-facing failure is gone: default 'br show', 'br sync --status', 'br doctor', and close/export workflows all succeed again. However, raw SQLite checks still report structural problems on both the old canonical DB and the rebuilt temp DB.\n\nObserved behavior on /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust:\n- 'br doctor --json' reports sqlite.integrity_check=ok\n- 'sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "pragma quick_check; pragma integrity_check;"' reports repeated 'NULL value in comments.created_at' plus missing rows from idx_comments_created_at / idx_comments_issue and idx_issues_list_active_order\n- the rebuilt temp DB created via 'br sync --import-only --rebuild --json --db .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260312T110100Z --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush' shows the same raw SQLite warnings\n\nThis suggests either:\n1. br doctor is masking/ignoring a class of integrity failures, or\n2. the JSONL import path reconstructs comments/index state that SQLite itself still considers invalid (possibly missing comment created_at backfill or index maintenance).\n\nFollow-up objective:\n- determine whether the warnings are benign legacy artifacts or a real latent bug in Beads import/doctor\n- identify why rebuilt DBs still inherit the issue from JSONL\n- make doctor and raw SQLite integrity surfaces agree, or explicitly document/waive the discrepancy if intentional\n\nUseful repro commands:\n- br doctor --json\n- sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "pragma quick_check; pragma integrity_check;"\n- sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "pragma table_info(comments);"\n- br sync --import-only --rebuild --json --db .beads/tmp.db --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush\n- sqlite3 .beads/tmp.db "pragma quick_check; pragma integrity_check;"","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:04:01.098240615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:05:43.035212272Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","integrity","tracking"]}
